name.description: The ID of the instance or a fully qualified identifier for the instance.

tier.description: The service tier of the instance.

memory_size_gb.description: The Redis memory size in GiB. Provision up to 300 GB of memory.

location_id.description: The zone where the instance will be provisioned. If not provided, the service will choose a zone for the instance. For STANDARD_HA tier, instances will be created across two zones for protection against zonal failures. If [alternative Location Id] is also provided, it must be different from [location Id]

alternative_location_id.description: Only applicable to STANDARD_HA tier which protects the instance against zonal failures by provisioning it across two zones. If provided, it must be a different zone from the one provided in [locationId].

redis_version.description: The version of Redis software. If not provided, latest supported version will be used. Currently, the supported values are REDIS_3_2 for Redis 3.2 compatibility,  REDIS_4_0 for Redis 4.0 compatibility (default), REDIS_5_0 for Redis 5.0 compatibility, REDIS_6_X for Redis 6.x compatibility, REDIS_7_0 for Redis 7.0 compatibility

display_name.description: An arbitrary and optional user-provided name for the instance.

reserved_ip_range.description: The CIDR range of internal addresses that are reserved for this instance. If not provided, the service will choose an unused /29 block, for example, 10.0.0.0/29 or 192.168.0.0/29. Ranges must be unique and non-overlapping with existing subnets in an authorized network.

labels.description: Resource labels to represent user provided metadata. Note: This field is non-authoritative, and will only manage the labels present in your configuration. Please refer to the field effective_labels for all of the labels present on the resource.

maintenance_policy.type: list(object({description: optional(string), weekly_maintenance_window: list(object({ day: string, start_time: list(object({ hours: optional(number), minutes: optional(number), seconds: optional(number), nanos: optional(number) }))}))}))
maintenance_policy.description: Maintenance policy for an instance. If no values are set, maintenance will be scheduled at midnight on the day selected in UTC

authorized_network.description: The full name of the Google Compute Engine network to which the instance is connected. If left unspecified, the default network will be used.

auth_enabled.description: Indicates whether OSS Redis AUTH is enabled for the instance.

connect_mode.description: The connection mode of the Redis instance.

persistence_config.type: list(object({persistence_mode: string, rdb_snapshot_period: optional(string), rdb_snapshot_start_time: optional(string)}))

transit_encryption_mode.description: The TLS mode of the Redis instance.

read_replicas_mode.description: Read replica mode. Can only be specified when trying to create the instance.

secondary_ip_range.description: Additional IP range for node placement. Required when enabling read replicas on an existing instance. For DIRECT_PEERING mode value must be a CIDR range of size /28, or "auto". For PRIVATE_SERVICE_ACCESS mode value must be the name of an allocated address range associated with the private service access connection, or "auto".

customer_managed_key.description: The KMS key reference that you want to use to encrypt the data at rest for this Redis instance. If this is provided, CMEK is enabled.
